response:
- Supports long-term cardiovascular and metabolic health by lowering resting blood pressure, regulating blood sugar levels, and reducing harmful LDL cholesterol, cutting the risk of common chronic conditions like type 2 diabetes, heart disease, and stroke by up to 35% per World Health Organization (WHO) research.
- Boosts mood and long-term cognitive function by triggering the release of endorphins (the body’s natural mood regulators) that reduce acute symptoms of anxiety and depression, while increasing cerebral blood flow to support the growth of new neural connections, lowering age-related risk of cognitive decline and dementia.
- Enhances long-term functional independence and quality of life by building and preserving lean muscle mass, bone density, and flexibility, which reduces fall and injury risk across all age groups, and supports the ability to complete routine daily tasks (from carrying groceries to climbing stairs) independently well into older adulthood.
